summary: Scientist specializing in translational oncology and drug response biology, combining hands-on bench expertise with computational rigor. Ph.D. in Biology with 4+ years of experience independently designing, executing, and troubleshooting large-scale cell-based and molecular studies — spanning mammalian cell culture, functional assays, and multi-omics platforms — to generate high-quality, reproducible data that has directly informed mechanisms of drug resistance and therapeutic target prioritization. Strong working knowledge of tumor biology and cell signaling pathways (NF-κB, TGF-β), paired with expertise in statistical modeling, multi-omics data integration, and machine learning (R, Python, SQL) to support data-driven decision-making in drug discovery and translational research. Skilled at communicating complex experimental and statistical insights to cross-functional teams; fluent in Mandarin and English.

items: Mammalian and cancer cell line culture (aseptic technique)
Cell viability, signaling, and functional assays
Tumor biology and cell signaling pathways (NF-κB, TGF-β, proteostasis)
Mechanisms of drug response and resistance
Protein network and complex analysis (e.g., CORUM-based)
Survival analysis (conceptual familiarity; extendable to clinical datasets)
Active learning and student-centered teaching strategies
Inclusive and equitable teaching practices for diverse student populations
Instructional technology integration (presentations, digital tools, data visualization)

experience.org: University of Washington — Schweppe Lab · Seattle, WA
bullets: Independently designed and executed a large-scale proteomic drug-perturbation platform across 24 human cancer cell lines and 15 therapeutics, generating and analyzing over 1,000 reproducible proteome and viability profiles to identify mechanisms of drug resistance

Performed cell-based functional assays and molecular techniques (Western blot, qPCR) to validate drug response phenotypes, and maintained mammalian/cancer cell line cultures with consistent aseptic technique across 1,000+ experimental samples

Identified drug-induced network rewiring and protein complex remodeling, linking cell signaling and stress-response pathways (NF-κB, proteostasis, TGF-β) to resistance and adaptive drug response relevant to oncology drug development
Developed predictive models (ElasticNet, LASSO, neural networks) to estimate drug sensitivity from molecular features, improving interpretability with SHAP-based feature importance

Built data pipelines and reproducible analysis workflows in Python and SQL for data cleaning, transformation, and statistical analysis (correlation modeling, variance analysis, feature selection) across multi-omics, multi-batch datasets
Created visualization tools and dashboards, and conducted literature reviews, to communicate findings to interdisciplinary collaborators and guide experimental direction

experience.org: University of Florida — Sixue Chen Lab · Gainesville, FL
bullets: Designed and executed hands-on experiments in molecular biology, biochemistry, and cell biology, independently troubleshooting assays to ensure reproducible data generation

Collected and analyzed experimental data using statistical and computational approaches — including differential expression and pathway-level analysis — with reproducible workflows in R and Python for data processing and statistical testing
Developed and documented standardized protocols (SOPs) to improve reproducibility and efficiency across experiments
Communicated experimental findings through peer-reviewed publications and presentations
